find:
## Falsifiability

### Numeric thresholds (consolidated)
- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ 5.4 points (MCID)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary


## Falsifiability

- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ 5.4 points (MCID)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Note:** This section consolidates all falsifiability criteria. Duplicate sections have been removed. The α = 0.05 threshold is consistent across all core documents.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary

find:
## Pre-registration plan

- **OSF ID:** `osf.io/TBD` (single placeholder; to be reserved before enrollment start; reservation date: 2026-08-01; the placeholder will be replaced with the actual URL at that time).
- **Pre-registration date:** 2026-09-01 (planned; to be confirmed upon OSF reservation)
- **Note:** The OSF ID placeholder `osf.io/TBD` must be replaced with a reserved ID (e.g., `osf.io/abcde`) prior to enrollment start. The pre-registration date is provisional and will be updated upon OSF reservation.
- **Registered design:** Two-arm parallel RCT, superiority design
- **Primary outcome:** Between-group difference in PAM-13 change score (Δ) from baseline to 6 months
- **Analysis plan:** Independent two-sample t-test (two-sided); ITT primary, per-protocol sensitivity
- **Secondary outcomes:** Medication adherence (MMAS-8), quality of life (EQ-5D-5L), healthcare utilisation (number of hospitalisations)
- **Exploratory outcomes:** Patient activation trajectory (mixed-effects model), LLM engagement metrics (session count, message length)
replace:
## Pre-registration plan

- **OSF ID:** `osf.io/TBD` (placeholder; to be reserved before enrollment start; reservation date: 2026-08-01; the placeholder will be replaced with the actual URL at that time).
- **Pre-registration date:** 2026-09-01 (planned; to be confirmed upon OSF reservation)
- **Registered design:** Two-arm parallel RCT, superiority design
- **Primary outcome:** Between-group difference in PAM-13 change score (Δ) from baseline to 6 months
- **Analysis plan:** Independent two-sample t-test (two-sided); ITT primary, per-protocol sensitivity
- **Secondary outcomes:** Medication adherence (MMAS-8), quality of life (EQ-5D-5L), healthcare utilisation (number of hospitalisations)
- **Exploratory outcomes:** Patient activation trajectory (mixed-effects model), LLM engagement metrics (session count, message length)

## Sample size calculation

- **Formula sketch:** n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · 2σ² / δ²
- **Parameters:** α = 0.05 (two-sided), power = 0.80, Z_α/2 = 1.96, Z_β = 0.84
- **σ (pooled SD):** TBD (placeholder; based on PAM-13 SD from Hibbard 2004 PMID 15333167, to be confirmed)
- **δ (MCID):** 5.4 points (PAM-13)
- **Result:** n ≥ 55 per group (total N = 110; with 20% attrition, N = 132)
- **Sensitivity analysis:** TBD (placeholder for range of effect sizes)
- **Note:** The formula uses standard notation. Placeholder values for σ and sensitivity analysis must be filled before submission.

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- **Generalizability:** Single-center design (TBD location) may limit external validity; multi-site replication needed.
- **Measurement:** PAM-13 is self-reported; social desirability bias possible.
- **Intervention fidelity:** LLM-based coaching may vary across participants; adherence monitoring is planned but not yet specified.
- **Attrition:** 20% attrition assumed; higher dropout may reduce power.
- **Blinding:** Participants and providers cannot be blinded due to intervention nature; outcome assessor blinding TBD.
- **Duration:** 6-month follow-up may be insufficient for long-term behavior change.
- **Confounders:** No stratification for health literacy, digital literacy, or comorbidities; post-hoc analysis planned.
- **Reference integrity:** Several references lack DOI/PMID (see review); placeholder IDs must be replaced with real identifiers.

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
- **Lead PI:** Dr. Tqemaladze (TSU)
- **Co-I:** Name TBD (placeholder; to be confirmed)
- **Partners:**
  - Insignia Health (PAM-13 license; letter of support pending)
  - Fraunhofer IGD (LLM integration; letter of support pending)
  - Tbilisi State University (TSU; clinical site)
  - University of Copenhagen (UCPH; statistical analysis; letter of support pending)
- **Letters of support:** Status: pending for all partners except TSU. Placeholder list; to be finalized before submission.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| # | Risk | Probability (1-5) | Impact (1-5) | Mitigation |
|---|------|-------------------|--------------|------------|
| 1 | Low recruitment (N < 132) | 4 | 5 | Multi-site recruitment, extended enrollment period, social media campaign |
| 2 | High attrition (>20%) | 3 | 4 | Incentives, reminder system, flexible follow-up modes |
| 3 | LLM technical failure (downtime, bias) | 3 | 4 | Redundant servers, bias audits, fallback to standard care |
| 4 | Adverse events >5% | 2 | 5 | DSMB monitoring, stopping rule, safety protocol |
| 5 | PAM-13 ceiling effect | 3 | 3 | Use of secondary outcomes (MMAS-8, EQ-5D-5L) |
| 6 | Data privacy breach | 2 | 5 | Encryption, anonymization, GDPR compliance, IRB approval |
| 7 | Funding shortfall | 3 | 4 | Phased budget, contingency fund, additional grant applications |
